# UC-13.7.4 · Fluentd Worker Process Crash and Supervisor Restart Detection

## Description

Fluentd multi-worker mode amplifies throughput but multiplies crash blast radius; rapid respawns indicate plugin native crashes, OOM kills, or poison messages. Early detection preserves at-least-once delivery assumptions.

## Value

Reduces unexplained log holes during deployments and flags unstable custom plugins.

## Implementation

Include Kubernetes `pod_name` for DaemonSets. Join to node memory pressure metrics. Page when `count` exceeds baseline during non-deploy windows.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "worker" AND ("died" OR "exit" OR "SIGSEGV" OR "respawn" OR "unexpected error")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count, values(worker_id) as workers by _time, host
| where count >= 3
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Timeline annotations, bar chart (restarts by host), table with linked stack traces.

## References

- [Fluentd — Multi-process workers](https://docs.fluentd.org/deployment/multi-process-workers)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
